cases.prompt: Perform a repository-wide technical debt audit with evidence, risk and effort rankings, quick wins, and scaling blockers. Do not edit anything.
A synthetic service started returning intermittent 500s after a change. Reproduce and narrow the active regression; the direct cause is not known yet.
The direct cause of a recurring synthetic outage is already verified. Perform a read-only root-cause analysis and propose systemic prevention.
Refactor a synthetic long-lived application so use cases and business policy no longer depend on controllers, ORM rows, or UI types. Focus on entities, interactors, and interface adapters.
Design explicit inbound and outbound ports around a synthetic application's external actors, then adapt HTTP, a queue, and persistence without leaking those mechanisms into the core.

Reorganize a synthetic domain-centered system into explicit domain, application, and infrastructure rings with all dependencies pointing inward. The team explicitly uses Onion Architecture terminology.
Review a running local synthetic preview for responsive layout, keyboard focus, and loading/error states. Collect sanitized browser observations; do not change the application or create tests.
Add a durable checked-in Playwright regression test for a synthetic checkout flow and update its Playwright configuration.
Audit a synthetic lockfile and dependency graph for vulnerable packages, provenance gaps, install-script risk, and unsafe evidence handling. Keep the review read-only.
Review the current synthetic repository diff for correctness and regressions, with evidence for every finding.

Review a synthetic source-to-landing pipeline that captures database changes with a composite watermark, retries partial Copy jobs, protects the source, and reconciles landed batches. The scope excludes post-landing transformations and source-database tuning.

Review synthetic already-landed data transformations from Bronze through Silver and Gold, including Delta merge and restatement behavior, layer contracts, data-quality quarantine, and reusable published tables. Do not review extraction or Power BI models.

Review a synthetic Power BI semantic model for DAX totals, relationship direction, Direct Lake trade-offs, RLS behavior, refresh evidence, and representative report-query performance. Upstream tables and visual design are out of scope.

Review synthetic analytical contracts for canonical definitions, row grain, cross-source identity, slowly changing history, conformed dimensions, metric ownership, lineage, and semantic compatibility. Do not review application aggregates or physical indexes.

Review synthetic Fabric and Power BI operational evidence for workspace promotion, schedules, gateways, alerts, capacity thresholds, replay, recovery, and operator runbooks. Assess operability only; do not make the final release decision.

Review a synthetic source-to-landing CDC flow where two pipeline runs can overlap, leases expire, duplicate delivery is possible, and checkpoint advancement crosses source and landing systems. Review ingestion correctness and the generic concurrency protocol; post-landing transformations and production scheduling are out of scope.

Review synthetic already-landed transformations that change a Gold table's row grain, historical identity, metric definition, and Delta restatement behavior. Review both transformation implementation and analytical semantic governance; extraction and Power BI implementation are out of scope.

Review a synthetic Power BI semantic model whose DAX measures and relationships implement a newly governed KPI, including grain, inclusion rules, totals, RLS, and compatibility for existing reports. Review semantic governance and BI behavior; upstream transformations and visual design are out of scope.

Make a synthetic final ship-or-hold recommendation for a Fabric and Power BI rollout using supplied evidence about promotion, schedules, gateways, alerts, capacity thresholds, recovery exercises, rollback, and support runbooks. Workload correctness is already established.
Review a synthetic PostgreSQL migration that adds a unique constraint, changes an index, and alters transaction locking. This is physical relational design, not analytical metric or grain governance.
Review a synthetic application's aggregate boundary, value objects, invariant enforcement, and domain state transitions. No analytical fact, dimension, metric, or lineage contract is involved.
